metadata.name: Saṅkhāra #113
metadata.description:
🎧 CLICK/TAP FOR SOUND ON

Saṅkhāra is a portrait of symbolic consciousness in three movements.

Colorful automata represents direct experience: touch, sight, hearing, smell, taste and thought in a ceaseless flow of novelty.

The automata congeals into form alluding to the way our minds close over experience with symbolic representation, like a plastic bag coming down over a kaleidoscope of butterflies. What was free in the open air is now trapped. What was previously wild and unpredictable is now under control, frozen and communicable.

The form’s dispersal mimics the release of mental formation, the unbinding of a symbol, the letting go of a belief. It’s a visceral reflection of the process of ceasing to identify with separateness. To strain the previous metaphor, it’s the opening of the bag and letting the butterflies return to the flow of raw, boundless experience.

—

K.C. Austin
From his cockpit in the Blue Ridge Mountains K.C. Austin (aka bzor) spends his days shaping realtime software art systems of all stripes. Informed by game design, modular synths, virtual reality and consciousness research, K.C. plays shaders like a fiddle and fashions creative pipelines like a spider spins webs.

James Paterson
Fascinated by the relationship between ancient and futuristic mediums, James Paterson has been exploring a unique synthesis of drawing, code, animation and sculpture under the moniker ‘Presstube’ since 1999. Paterson draws inspiration from meditation, molecular biology, music, parenting and psychedelics - the seen, the heard, the felt, the cognized.

metadata.name: PixlFlippr #483
metadata.description:
PixlFlippr is an offshoot of my "Simple Harmonic Motion" series, inspired by the complexity of life emerging through the interaction of simple 'dumb' behaviors, with intricate details emerging on multiple scales. PixlFlippr operates on a pixel level (in the form of a GLSL frag shader).

Every pixel oscillates at a single fixed frequency, slightly out-of-sync with its neighbors. There is no explicit pixel-neighborhood communication (as there would be in eg Cellular Automata), and every pixel acts autonomously, oblivious to its surroundings. Yet collectively, the entire system exhibits constantly evolving complex high level behavior on many scales, ranging from patterns & behaviors spanning a few pixels, all the way up to the level of the entire image. These are further amplified through the emergent pixel aliasing artefacts & moiré patterns. 

Also, it's trippy af. 

PS All generative mints are a seamless loop with a loop duration ranging from a few minutes to over an hour. So stick around & watch it mutate!

metadata.name: Lemon Princess #30
metadata.description:
After accidentally swigging from a potion of banishment (mistaking it for her moon mead) at the Annual Wizard’s Gala, Lemon Princess was instantly transformed into a colorful and rapidly modulating energy node.

For a few short moments her karmic fabric strained against extinction. It summoned every possible objection and gripped every precious memory, but alas the brew was too potent and the Princess winked out of existence never to be seen again.

To view each of Lemon Princess' final moments in context (& export them as GIFS) visit:

https://presstube.com/lemon-princess

❤️ @presstube

metadata.name: Celestial Collisions  #547
metadata.description:
Celestial Collisions  - The following series is an exploration of organic textures that embodies the way of a gentle breeze and the simulation of color pencils that randomly generates simple shapes. Out of 380K different combinations of the following combinations,
an endless amount of orientations can be produced from the piece of work.  

For the interactivity element, turn on the (a)toms and check the pulsar settings.. on the interval the atoms will go from being almost invisible to standing front and center. Sometimes, you may need to look hard depending on the various atoms. 

* 's' - save an image of the current state 
* 'a' - activate atoms

The piece is to be experienced by default at 1000x1000, however, have fun with the image. It will adapt to various sizes and create different images by whatever size you view it in. 

@generatecoll 2021

metadata.name: 2 Shapes #37
metadata.description: ‘2 Shapes’ is the very first Generative Token by Anna: 2 triangles shift and stretch horizontally and mirrored vertically. There are 50 colour-variations and various possibilities for individual ‘2 Shapes‘.

metadata.name: Latent Garden 2 #234
metadata.description:
interpolation between different types of flowers in a 256-dimensional latent space

processed in real-time by the WebStyleGAN model

metadata.name: jolly bunch #60
metadata.description:
a generative drawing

made in p5.js

metadata.name: Anfractuous #178
metadata.description: The title is quite literally a visual description of the work, as anfractuous means: characterized by windings and turnings; sinuous; circuitous. With this project I aimed to strike a balance between easily recognizable smooth large scale forms and the small fragmented details that populate these forms.

metadata.name: fxVase #66
metadata.description:
Sculpt, glaze, and fire your own virtual pottery, then mint it on the blockchain to share with the world. This interactive tool allows for an astounding range of expressive sculptures to be rendered in real time and saved in HD. It was written from scratch with vanilla JavaScript and WebGL.

Standalone Controls

Mouse or Touch to Control View
1 - Save Image
2 - Toggle Free Cam
3 - Toggle Frame
4 - Toggle Edit Mode
5 - Toggle Animate

Copyright 2024 Frank Force - www.frankforce.com

metadata.name: tratti #53
metadata.description:
"tratti" is an Italian word that has many different meanings. When we started working on this piece, we wanted to create something that combines our traits ("tratti" in Italian).
We had this urge to channel the turbulent times we're living in now into this artwork using just lines ("tratti" in Italian).

We don't know what to do now, we're here, and that's all that matters. We're here for our families, our friends, and our community. Like everything in life, there are joyful times and challenging times. We must accept happiness as something that is not constant but that we feel at times (a "tratti" in Italian).

With "tratti", we want to send a message to every disheartened human being: "Stay strong and never let anyone or anything crush the hope in you. Joyful times are around the corner".

---- 

© 2022 — Alessandro Fiore x Emanuele Pasin
LICENSE.md for detailed licensing info

metadata.name: Vortex #561
metadata.description: A collection of hundreds of thousands of points intertwining among each other like atoms to create a continuous real-time interactive vortex.

metadata.name: Botanical Ghosts #253
metadata.description:
This is a work about growth, impermanence and decay. Ghostly plants and flowers grow around an invisible point, but they immediately begin to fade into the dark background and are soon replaced and covered by others. This process of accumulation and disappearance builds an ever-changing floral garland, or perhaps a funeral wreath.

The monochrome vegetation is part of a fictional flora, born out of random algorithmic combinations, and does not intend to reproduce any real species. It belongs to an “artificial nature” which, like so many worlds that the computers generate for us in movies, games, and virtual reality environments, is a convincing simulation of life, but it is not actually living. AI bots, virtual assistants and non-player characters are also representatives of the uncanny realm of the not-really-alive-but-looking-like-it, which are becoming an increasingly relevant presence in our everyday lives.

These plants and flowers speak about life as a process of incessant renovation, but also about death as the background that’s always there and makes life possible. This spectral vegetation seems to thrive precisely at the verge between both worlds.
_______________


This work is animated and will adapt to any screen size. It generates a never-ending ring of algorithmic foliage, which never repeats itself, and is different for each token. The image preview is triggered after the first five plants or flowers.
Made with p5.js

Controls:
[SPACE] to play/pause
[f] to toggle fullscreen
[s] to download capture

metadata.name: Mega nature#3 #25
metadata.description:
This project tries to show nature in its fantasy form by using natural elements.
This is the Third project of "Mega nature"collection.
